self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can carry this bounded Architecture slice forward into direct adoption without reopening the prior Engine, handoff, and post-consumption evidence chain by hand.
self_improvement.mechanism: The bounded closeout now records an adoption-ready Architecture result and paired decision artifact directly on the bounded-result record.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this closeout, the reopened bounded slice still depended on manual interpretation to decide whether it could move forward as retained Directive-owned Architecture output.
self_improvement.expected_effect: The bounded result 'ESLint is the first outside source in the current pressure loop that isolates a concrete Architecture artifact instead of only general workflow guidance. The bounded retained slice is one Directive-owned checker-definition schema pattern for repo validation scripts: each checker should declare rule identity, purpose, option-shape validation, severity posture, suppression boundary, evidence contract, and self-test entrypoint. Out of scope: ESLint AST visitor runtime, plugin packaging, JavaScript-specific execution, editor integration, bulk-suppression storage mechanics, and any host/runtime execution seam. The next truthful move is one implementation-target slice that materializes this schema against a very small subset of existing check scripts.' can now move directly into the normal adoption/materialization path as Architecture-owned output.

decision.rationale: The mechanism is not adoption-ready yet; keep it experimental until readiness and evidence gaps are closed.
decision.stay_experimental_reason: Required proof has not been executed yet.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
